label: Upload information
description: Enable users to provide required information by uploading documents or submitting several related items together, rather than answering individual questions.
purpose: Collect evidence, records, or structured information in one place to support an application or request.
use_when: Information is provided as files, attachments, or grouped items.
Data is imported from another system or generated by the service rather than manually entered.
Information is submitted in bulk or as a single action.
do_not_use_when: Information can be captured clearly and efficiently through individual question pages.
The page only provides guidance without requiring user action.

outputs.label: Uploaded files or items
outputs.description: The files or data items submitted by the user.

variants.label: Alternative submission method
variants.description: Used when users cannot upload digitally. Explains alternative routes (assisted, postal, in-person). Service records that alternative submission was selected.
use_when: Technical, accessibility, or connectivity constraints prevent online submission.
Documents exist only in physical form.

ux_considerations.text: Explain accepted formats, size limits, and what makes a document suitable — without overloading the user.
Enable the user to review, remove, or replace items before confirming.
Errors must not force users to re-submit information already successfully provided.
Allow users to save progress and return without losing submitted information.
The interface must confirm successful and secure receipt without displaying file contents.

technical_notes.text: Protect uploaded information during upload, import, and storage using appropriate security controls.
File names, previews, and metadata must not expose personal or confidential details.
Inform users if their information will be shared with other systems or organisations.
Validate file type, size, format, and completeness both client-side and server-side.
